summary: Strong, well-structured contract with 24 criteria covering all three sprint features. Criteria are specific, testable, and appropriately scoped. Safety considerations are thorough and aligned with CLAUDE.md restrictions. The out-of-scope boundaries are reasonable and well-justified. Minor observations noted below but none warrant revision.

shellCompletions_3_14.notes: Covers --install-completion, --show-completion, zsh script content validation, and command name presence. The out-of-scope exclusion of dynamic account/mailbox completions and bash/fish shells is well-justified — Typer's built-in completion covers commands and options, which is what the spec requires for v1.

helpTextQuality.notes: Seven criteria covering: all 13 commands have help text, all options have descriptions (spot-check), safety messaging for compose/reply/forward and --dangerously-send, top-level overview, style consistency, and default value documentation. Comprehensive.

testingInfrastructure_3_15.notes: Ten criteria covering: test file structure, 5 specific integration tests (accounts, mailboxes, messages, doctor, JSON output), pytest default exclusion, explicit -m inclusion, documentation, and timeouts. Matches the product spec scope exactly.

regression.notes: Unit test suite pass, command availability, and safety model integrity. Combined with regressionSprints: [1-9], provides full regression coverage.

criteriaAssessment.notes: Both flags already appear in current --help output (they are Typer built-ins). This criterion verifies they survive the sprint's changes — valid as a baseline check.
Good thresholds: non-empty (≥10 lines) and contains zsh-specific keywords (compdef, _mailctl, compadd). Specific enough to catch a broken completion script.
All 8 command/group names verified in completion script output. Correctly lists: accounts, mailboxes, messages, drafts, compose, reply, forward, doctor — matches the actual CLI.
Minor observation: --install-completion modifies the user's shell config (.zshrc). This is a real side effect but acceptable since Typer's implementation is idempotent. Evaluator should be aware.
13-command list verified against actual CLI: accounts list, mailboxes list, messages list/show/search/mark/move/delete, compose, reply, forward, drafts edit, doctor. All confirmed present.
Spot-check approach is practical. The 5 chosen commands (messages list, compose, messages mark, messages search, doctor) cover high-option-count commands where missing help text is most likely.
Clear and important safety criterion. Currently all three commands already mention 'draft' prominently in their help text — this verifies the sprint doesn't regress that.
Good complementary criterion to C-256. Verifies the dangerous flag's help text is appropriately alarming.
Tests the top-level overview quality. All 8 entries confirmed present in current CLI output.
Code-inspection method is appropriate for style consistency. The threshold (sentence case, no ALL-CAPS, consistent voice) is specific enough to evaluate objectively.
Spot-check of 3 options with defaults. Reasonable — ensures help text is informative, not just present.
Structural criterion — test files exist with proper markers. Foundation for C-262 through C-266.
Read-only integration test. Safe to execute against real Mail.app per CLAUDE.md.
Read-only integration test. Safe to execute.
Good inclusion of empty-INBOX handling. Ensures the test doesn't fail on a clean mailbox.
Read-only diagnostic command. Safe to execute.
Tests JSON output structure from real Mail.app, not just format. Good for catching AppleScript parsing issues that mocked tests might miss.
Verified: pyproject.toml already has addopts = "-m 'not integration'". This criterion confirms the config survives the sprint.
Threshold of ≥5 integration tests is tight but achievable — criteria C-262 through C-266 each require at least one test function.
Four-point documentation checklist is specific and objective.
10-60 second range is well-calibrated for real Mail.app communication.
Full unit test regression. The 'no tests removed' check is a good guard against accidental deletions.
Smoke test for all prior sprint commands.
Critical safety regression. Tests that no safety model tests were modified or removed.

outOfScopeAssessment: All 8 out-of-scope items are well-justified. Dynamic completions for account/mailbox names are correctly deferred (osascript at completion time adds latency and complexity). Bash/fish exclusion aligns with v1 scope. Integration tests correctly limited to read-only operations. Write-operation integration tests and --dangerously-send tests are absolutely forbidden per CLAUDE.md.
safetyAssessment: Safety notes are thorough and correctly aligned with CLAUDE.md restrictions. Integration tests are all read-only (accounts list, mailboxes list, messages list, doctor). Completion commands don't interact with Mail.app. Help text changes are string-only. The evaluator safety boundary is clearly drawn.
regressionAssessment: All prior sprints (1-9) correctly listed. C-271 covers unit test suite, C-272 covers command availability, C-273 covers safety model integrity. Combined with the formal regression sprint list, this is comprehensive.

concerns.description: C-253 (--install-completion zsh) has a real side effect: it modifies ~/.zshrc. Typer's implementation is idempotent, so this is safe, but the evaluator should know this writes to the user's shell config.
C-250 tests for flags that already exist (Typer provides them by default). This is more of a regression check than a new feature test, which is fine for a polish sprint.
